goal: Red-team the existing Time Capsule form, data, and safety contracts before the parent Refine run authors an implementation-ready non-executed plan.
context: The current package is plan-ready only for a non-collecting form and synthetic fixtures. Real intake lacks a selected application stack, production database contract, credential governance, sealed-data threat model, moderation policy, incident ownership, and long-term stewardship. Reviewers attack the same local source packet and write cited findings only; they do not implement fixes or approve launch.
p1_trigger: synthesis of three independent risk surfaces
anti_bias_global: whether inviting participation, durable correctness, and fail-safe privacy can coexist without one surface silently weakening another

predicted_disagreements: form-accessibility runs a scope attack while database-reliability runs counter-examples on the attack-vector axis; form may accept a comprehensible bounded journey that data rejects until transaction and recovery boundaries close, so data exposes product optimism.

form-accessibility runs a scope attack while privacy-safety runs a definitional attack on the attack-vector axis; form may favor an inviting sealed choice that safety rejects when consent or confidentiality language exceeds its guarantees, so safety exposes engagement bias.

database-reliability runs counter-examples while privacy-safety runs a definitional attack on the attack-vector axis; data may accept correctly durable storage that safety rejects as unnecessary or harmful retention, so safety exposes correctness-without-minimization bias and data exposes safety claims without operable state.

agents.angle: scope attack: test whether the complete participant journey is understandable, voluntary, accessible, and honest about public, sealed, quiet, edit, close, receipt, and release consequences
counter-example attack: use concurrency, replay, partial failure, retention, migration, backup, restore, and close-time cases to break the API and database state model
definitional attack: challenge every privacy, sealed, safety, consent, moderation, incident, recovery, and ten-year stewardship promise against explicit guarantees and tail failures
